pattern: Each merge just deletes an internal sign and pushes its per-km time onto the next surviving sign, so the problem reduces to picking which k internal signs to drop; a DP over the last two kept signs and the removal count finds the cheapest choice.
hints: The first and last signs can never be removed, and a merge only ever folds a sign's time into the sign immediately to its right.
After all merges the kept signs are a subsequence keeping endpoints; the segment leaving a kept sign costs its distance times the summed original times of the signs merged into it.

Run a DP over (previous kept sign, current kept sign, number removed so far): the segment leaving the current sign uses the time accumulated from the previous kept sign, and you advance to the next kept sign paying that segment, requiring exactly k removals at the end.

edge_inputs.description: one merge of the cheap middle pair
one merge among four interior segments
zero merges leaves the road unchanged
must remove the only removable interior sign
two merges collapse most interior signs
merging the wider segment is worse than the narrow one

stress.description: random increasing positions and small times with a modest merge count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 14))
    pts = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 100000), n - 1))
    position = [0] + pts
    l = position[-1]
    cap = 100
    time = []
    remaining = cap
    for i in range(n):
        slots = n - i
        hi = max(1, min(8, remaining - (slots - 1)))
        t = rng.randrange(1, hi + 1)
        time.append(t)
        remaining -= t
    k = rng.randrange(0, min(n - 2, 6) + 1) if n > 2 else 0
    return [l, n, k, position, time]
stress.note: n and k kept small so the combinations oracle stays fast and sum(time) <= 100
